heading: Welcome to Last Call RP
text: Last Call RP is a whitelisted, 18+ roleplay community built for people who want quality RP without all the drama. We're here to have fun, tell great stories, and build a city worth living in. These rules keep things fair for everyone at the bar.

heading: 1. The Golden Rule
text: Do not DM STAFF OR OWNER, FOR ANY REASON. Respect everyone — players, staff, streamers, newcomers. We're all here to have fun. Treat people how you want to be treated, or find a different server. Don't be a rule lawyer. If you're looking for a loopho

heading: 2. Age & Identity
text: You must be 18+ to play. No exceptions, no workarounds. One character account per person. Don't share accounts. Your forum name must match or clearly link to your in-game name. You should have a well thought out backstory for your character. Using A.

heading: 3. Communication Standards
text: Keep OOC (out-of-character) chat clean and minimal in-game. Use Discord for OOC discussions. No racist, sexist, homophobic, or otherwise hateful language — in-character OR out. There's no 'my character is racist' exception. No doxxing, real-world thr

heading: 4. Whitelisting & Whitelisted Jobs
text: Some jobs (police, EMS, DOJ, etc.) require a whitelist application and approval. Don't show up in a cop uniform without one. Whitelisted faction members are expected to be active and maintain conduct standards. Inactivity without notice may result in

heading: 5. Streaming & Recording
text: You're welcome to stream Last Call RP. Don't use your stream to gain IC advantages (stream sniping = ban). Don't break scenes to wave at your camera. Keep it immersive. If someone asks not to appear on stream, respect it and keep them off-cam.

heading: 6. Bans & Punishments
text: Punishments range from verbal warnings to permanent bans depending on severity. Ban evasion with alternate accounts results in a permanent ban on all accounts. You can appeal any ban through our Discord ticket system. Be respectful in appeals — hosti

heading: What We Expect from Every Player
text: Good RP isn't just about knowing the rules — it's about adding to the world, not subtracting from it. Every interaction should leave things more interesting than you found them.

heading: 1. Fear RP
text: Your character values their life. When there's a gun in your face, act like it. You cannot ignore threats to your life just because you think you could 'win' the situation OOC. Running from someone actively pointing a firearm at you (within 10 feet)

heading: 2. New Life Rule (NLR)
text: After your character is downed and revived by EMS (or respawns), they have fuzzy memories of the events leading to their death. You cannot return to active combat zones 15 minutes after being revived. You cannot use OOC knowledge from before your dea

heading: 3. Powergaming
text: Powergaming = forcing unrealistic outcomes or using mechanics in ways that deny others meaningful roleplay. Don't use /me to make other characters do things (e.g., '/me grabs John's gun' — you don't get to decide what happens to John's gun). Don't ex

heading: 4. Metagaming
text: Information from Discord, Twitch streams, or OOC chats cannot be used in-character. Radio channels are IC. Don't create private Discord calls to relay IC information . This includes weekly meetings for organizations. Churches should be held IC only a

heading: 5. Random Deathmatch (RDM) & Vehicle Deathmatch (VDM)
text: RDM: Don't attack or kill someone without a valid RP reason. Boredom is not a valid reason. VDM: Don't use your vehicle as a weapon . There must be prior RP engagement before violence. Drive-bys on strangers with zero prior interaction = instant puni

heading: 6. Combat Logging
text: Don't disconnect to avoid being robbed, arrested, killed, or facing any other IC consequence. If you crash mid-scene, return ASAP and notify the other parties. Unscheduled s erver restarts don't count — just resume where you left off.

heading: 7. RP Continuity & Scene Endings
text: Don't break character in the middle of active scenes without a genuine emergency. If you need to pause a scene, try your absolute best to RP a reason for it, ie your character needs to use the bathroom, take a phone call. Make use of emotes if you ha

heading: 8. Sensitive RP Topics
text: Last Call RP allows darker RP themes as part of storytelling — but with guardrails. Sexual RP between characters requires both players' explicit OOC consent. And must be done in the privacy of your house. Sexual RP involving minors is an immediate pe

heading: Why Vehicle Rules Matter
text: Cars are one of the most common sources of RP issues on any server. Whether it's a pursuit, a crash, or just parking on someone's lawn, this doc covers what's expected behind the wheel.

heading: 1. Drive Realistically
text: Drive at speeds that make sense for the road and situation. Nobody in real life is doing 200mph through downtown. Obey traffic laws when there's no active RP reason not to. Red lights, stop signs, lane discipline — it all matters. Don't treat the cit

heading: 2. Vehicle Deathmatch (VDM)
text: Do NOT use your vehicle as a weapon . Hit-and-runs on players require active, ongoing RP justification. Ramming police vehicles during a chase must have a proportionate in-character reason. Pinning someone on foot with a vehicle to rob/detain them is

heading: 3. Vehicle Pursuits
text: Pursuit rules for LEOs are defined in the LEO SOP. Civilians: use good judgment and don't intentionally bait chases. Helicopter griefing (hovering over a scene just to be annoying) is a violation.

heading: 4. Storing & Spawning Vehicles
text: Don't spawn or de-spawn vehicles in the middle of active RP scenes. Vehicles left in a public space are subject to being towed by police or mechanics. Garages are the only valid spawn location for personal vehicles. No summoning a car from thin air i

heading: 5. Illegal Modifications
text: Custom illegal modifications (nitrous, illegal plates, weapons mounts ) exist in-lore but require IC acquisition through the right channels. Cosmetic mods are fine — go wild with the wrap.

heading: 6. Emergency Vehicles
text: Civilian players cannot drive emergency vehicles (police cars, ambulances, fire trucks) without whitelisted faction approval. If you steal an emergency vehicle for RP purposes, it must be part of a real scene — not just joyriding in a cop car. Yield

heading: 7. Aircraft
text: Flying requires a pilot's license obtained through in-game means . Don't fly over active police scenes, robberies, or chases to stream-snipe or relay info. That's metagaming. Restricted airspace (military base, prison, etc.) is off-limits without spe

heading: The Spirit of Criminal RP
text: Criminal RP is some of the most fun you can have on this server. Heists, gangs, drug operations, turf wars — it's all on the table. But it only works when criminals play with purpose, not just to grief. A good criminal leaves a mark on the city. A ba

heading: 1. Initiation & Setup
text: You must verbally initiate before using violence. 'Get on the ground or I shoot' is an initiation. Running up and punching someone is not. Initiation applies to your group. If Player A initiates with Player B's crew, the whole crew is engaged — but s

heading: 2. Robberies
text: Player robberies: you can rob players of cash, items, and vehicles with proper RP. DO NOT pocket wipe i.e. removing phone s, removing food and drinks. Don't rob the same player more than once per 24-hour period. Let people breathe. Th e exception to

heading: 3. Hostage Rules
text: Hostages must be treated as people, not furniture. Engage with them. Give them a reason to be scared. You cannot kill a hostage unless negotiations have fully broken down and you've given fair warning. Hostages cannot be held for longer than 45 minut

heading: 4. Gangs & Turf
text: Gang membership must be roleplayed — not just declaring yourself a gang with no backstory or structure. Gangs must be recognised by the Government to be official. Turf wars need IC history. You can't declare war on a gang you've never met. Drive-bys

heading: 5. Drugs & Contraband
text: Drug manufacturing and selling is an active RP process — you don't just press a button. Create scenes around supply, demand, and risk. Drug spots are contested IC. If someone muscled you off a corner, deal with it in-character. Don't use drug mechani

heading: 6. Rule of Engagement — When Things Go Wrong
text: If you're caught, you're caught. Accept the RP consequence. Don't RDM your way out of an arrest. Dying during a criminal operation triggers NLR — you don't remember the heist, the crew, or who shot you , unless you are taken to the hospital, then the

heading: 7. Cop Baiting
text: Deliberately trying to start a police chase with zero meaningful RP is considered baiting and will be handled. Doing donuts outside the PD, honking at officers, or dressing as a cop to confuse dispatch are all examples of low-effort baiting. If you w

heading: Meet the Staff Team
text: Staff at Last Call RP are volunteers who keep the lights on and the peace between scenes. They're players too — they have characters, crews, and stories. The difference is they're also responsible for making sure everyone gets a fair shot at good RP.

heading: 1. Staff Roles
text: Owner: Final say on all policy, direction, and appeals. Rarely in admin sits — they're the last line. Senior Admin: Oversees staffing, major ban reviews, and policy updates. Admin: Handles ban enforcement, appeals, and escalated player issues. Modera

heading: 2. Admin Sits
text: An admin sit is a moderated out-of-character meeting to resolve a dispute or report. All parties must be respectful — yes, even if you think you're right. Don't call admin sits to win an argument. Call them for genuine rule violations. Lying in an ad

heading: 3. Reporting a Player
text: Use the #player-report channel or in-game /report to file reports. Include: your name, their name, what happened, when it happened, and evidence if available. Don't report the same person multiple times for the same incident. One report, let staff ha

heading: 4. Appealing Decisions
text: Bans, warnings, and faction removals can all be appealed through the Discord ticket system. Appeals must be submitted within 7 days of the action taken. Be specific and calm in your appeal. 'This is unfair' is not an argument. Explain what happened f

heading: 5. Staff Conduct Standards
text: Staff are held to the same rules as everyone else — plus a bit more. Staff cannot abuse their position for IC advantages (spawning items, teleporting unfairly, looking at player locations). Staff must recuse themselves from situations involving their

heading: 6. Contacting Staff
text: For urgent in-game issues: use /report in game. For general questions and ticket-based issues: Discord is your go-to. Don't DM staff individually about reports or bans unless they've asked you to. Use the proper channels so it's tracked. Don't ping s

heading: 7. Staff Availability
text: Staff are volunteers. Response times vary. Peak hours see faster responses — off-peak may take longer. If something is urgent and no staff are available, document it (screenshot/video) and file a report as soon as someone's on. Staff aren't here to p
